description_long: This office building features a tall, rectangular main structure with a uniform grid of windows on its front facade, complemented by a much taller, slender tower attached to one side. The main building is clad in a beige or light brown material, likely concrete or stone, with regularly spaced horizontal and vertical window bands. The base of the building includes a recessed entry area and a dark ground-level section, possibly indicating a lobby or commercial frontage. The side and rear facades are largely windowless, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The attached tower, possibly a service or elevator shaft, rises significantly above the main structure and is finished in a matching material. The overall appearance is functional and modern, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on verticality.
description: A modern, beige office building with a tall, slender tower attached to one side and a grid of windows on the main facade. The design is functional, with a recessed entry and minimal exterior decoration.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main facade with a grid of windows, a recessed entry, and the attached tall tower on the left.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the extreme height of the attached tower and the narrow profile of the main building.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a mostly blank wall with no visible windows, emphasizing the building's utilitarian side.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the full height of the tower and the solid, windowless side of the main structure.
attribute_window_pattern: Regular horizontal bands of rectangular windows, evenly spaced across the main facade, with no windows on the side or rear walls.
